Title: Evolution of metabolic novelty: A trichome-expressed invertase creates specialized metabolic diversity in wild tomato

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Acylglucose biosynthesis from acylsucrose precursors in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of P-type acylsucroses to acylglucoses
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: P-type acylsucroses (pyranose ring-acylated acylsucroses), including S3:19, S3:15, S3:22
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the glycosidic bond of pyranose ring-acylated P-type acylsucroses to remove the fructofuranose moiety and generate corresponding acylglucoses; inactive on F-type acylsucroses and unmodified sucrose under the reported assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for acylglucose production in the presence of P-type acylsucrose precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protective specialized metabolites; candidate natural pesticides mediating environmental interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes and is necessary/sufficient for acylglucose accumulation from P-type acylsucrose precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL-guided candidate identification; trichome promoter-reporter assay; qRT-PCR; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in Solanum pennellii; stable transgenic complementation/expression in BIL6180; in vitro enzyme assay with purified recombinant protein; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpASFF1 (Sopen03g040490) from the chromosome 3 AG3.2 locus is a GH32 trichome-expressed invertase-like enzyme linked to acylglucose accumulation. 2. CRISPR-Cas9 loss-of-function mutants in Solanum pennellii LA0716 abolished detectable acylglucoses while retaining acylsucroses, demonstrating necessity. 3. Expression of SpASFF1 under its native promoter in the P-type acylsucrose-producing Solanum lycopersicum introgression line BIL6180 caused accumulation of G3:15 and G3:22, demonstrating sufficiency in vivo when precursor substrates are present. 4. Recombinant SpASFF1 hydrolyzed purified P-type S3:19 to G3:19 in vitro but showed no activity toward F-type S3:22 or sucrose, establishing substrate specificity. 5. SpASFF1 acts specifically on pyranose ring-acylated acylsucroses, explaining why acylglucoses accumulate only in backgrounds producing P-type rather than F-type acylsucroses.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in Solanum pennellii LA0716 glandular trichome tip cells; transcript levels were 3.7-fold higher in trichomes than in underlying stem tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Solanum lycopersicum M82, ASFF1 transcripts were lower in trichomes than in shaved stem tissue; transgenic proSpASFF1 reporter drove expression in type I/IV trichome tip cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf/stem surface glandular trichomes, especially type I/IV trichome tip cells
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SpASFF1 promoter from Solanum pennellii LA0716 (1.8-kb upstream region), trichome tip cell-expressed; 2x35S used only for Cas9 in editing construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown under controlled growth chamber or greenhouse conditions; acylglucose accumulation depended on genetic background producing P-type rather than F-type acylsucroses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcript abundance by qRT-PCR; no public omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong engineering target to create acylglucose-producing tomato lines, but effective deployment requires precursor pathway context that generates P-type acylsucroses; can be stacked with SpASAT2 and SpASAT3 or edits that remove furanose-ring acylation. Useful for engineering protective acylsugar diversity and potential natural pesticide traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct growth or yield penalty reported; metabolic outcome is contingent on precursor availability and substrate specificity, so expression alone in F-type acylsucrose backgrounds is insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper renames Sopen03g040490 as ASFF1. SpASFF1 is described as a derived β-fructofuranosidase co-opted from general metabolism into specialized metabolism. It did not hydrolyze unmodified sucrose in vitro, whereas yeast invertase did. The paper emphasizes a three-gene epistatic system in which P-type acylsucrose biosynthesis by SpASAT2/SpASAT3 enabled SpASFF1-mediated acylglucose formation.
